main_ccd.name: Alex
main_ccd.relevant_history: Grew up with a highly critical parent. Several past attempts at change (diets, courses, therapy) were abandoned partway, each reinforcing a sense of failure.
intermediate_beliefs: If I set a goal and fail, it proves I am incompetent.
I should avoid situations where I might fail.
coping_strategies: Dismissing plans before they can be tested.

mesc_sentences: I've tried making plans before and they never stick.
Honestly, I don't really see the point in setting goals.
Whenever I fail at something, it just confirms what I already think about myself.

dialogue_excerpt.content: It sounds like planning has felt discouraging in the past. Could we look at one small, manageable goal together?
Goals? I don't think they work. I always fail. It just makes me feel worse. Can we skip this?
I hear that setting goals brings up a lot of pressure for you.
Yeah. What's the point if I'm just going to give up anyway.
